person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
Biography
Marriages and children
Children of Sybil and Pain. Sybil, the widow of Pain fitz John, who died in 1137, then married Josce Dinan.

Sybil and Pain had two daughters, no sons.

1.) Cecily. Her first husband was Roger, the son of Miles of Gloucester. Pain arranged Cecily and Roger's marriage. The marriage contract specified that Roger would inherit all of Pain's lands, but as result of the latter's death the marriage was not contracted until December 1137, when King Stephen confirmed the terms of the settlement.

2.) Agnes. Agnes married Warin de Munchensy and after his death Haldenald de Bidun. She died after 1185, by which time she was described as a widow.
Children of Sybil and Josce.

Sibil, who married Hugh de Pulgenet and died in 1212
Hawise Dinan, b. c. 1146

Parents
There is some uncertainty concerning Sybil's parents. The leading scholarly theory is now that she was the daughter of Hugh de Lacy. The older proposal was that his wife Adelicia de Talbot.

There are two main proposals, but most historians agree that Sybil was closely related to the Talbots and Lacys...

For one thing, Geoffrey's barony ended up in the hands of the two heiresses of Sibil (and her first husband, Pain fitz John).
Secondly, Geoffrey II Talbot was recorded in the the Gesta Stephani, as a "cognatus" of Gilbert de Lacy, which implies a blood relationship, so explanations also need to be at least compatible with this. (However, the exact relationship of Gilbert to the other de Lacys is also uncertain! See the third post of Phillips, mentioned below.)

1. Daughter of Hugh de Lacy, and niece of Geoffrey Talbot. This was argued by Wightman, and is widely accepted by published historians now, including Keats-Rohan, Judith Green, Paul Dalton. This has been usefully summarized and criticized by Chris Phillips on SGM.

The main evidence of Wightman is that "Her daughter Cecily, later countess of Hereford, described Hugh I as her grandfather. [Cart. Ewyas Harold, fos. 153, 153b; Cart. Llanthony A 1, I, 11; XVI, 2.]" NOTE: as Phillips wrote: "If there's really watertight evidence that Cecily called her grandfather Hugh de Lacy, that would be a fatal blow to CP's suggestion."

And as an explanation of how this could be compatible with the above-mentioned relationship between Geoffrey II Talbot and Gilbert de Lacy, Keats-Rohan (DP p.231) suggests that Geoffrey II's sister was the wife Hugh de Lacy, and the mother of Sibil. Chris Phillips finds this unconvincing, but Keats-Rohan's main point is only that "clearly there was a link between the Talbots and the Lacys".

2. Daughter of Geoffrey Talbot, and niece of Hugh de Lacy. This was the previous standard argument, given in Complete Peerage Vol.9, p.425 under MUNCHENSY. There are at least two bits of circumstantial evidence in support of this:

Geoffrey was recorded as having a daughter named Sibil.

Cecilia, named above, was once recorded under the name "Cecilia Talbot", in a grant to Begham Priory. But note that all the scholars involved admit to the flexible use of second names in this period. There is no proposal by anyone that she is the daughter of a Talbot father!

Similarly to the first proposal, Complete Peerage also includes a speculation about a connection between the two families. In this case they make Sibil's mother the sister of Geoffrey I Talbot, making Sibil a niece of Geoffrey I and a first cousin of Geoffrey II.

NOTE: any decision about Sibil's parents should therefore effect other profiles where a link is shown between Talbots and Lacys, in order to be consistent. All those speculative links are only supporting arguments to the discussion about Sibil.

1139 Sybil Held Ludlow Castle
As explained on the Wikipedia article for Josce de Dinan, King Stephen apparently made sure that after the death of Pain fitz John, Sibil should remarry to a suitable husband who could be responsible for this important castle.

zh.lifeSummary: 當Sybil Talbot於大約1100年出生在英國英格蘭賀福爾艾維亞斯時，她的父親Geoffrey I Talbot of Swanscombe32歲，母親Agnes de Lacy26歲。她於大約1125年與Payn fitz John結婚。他們至少是2個女兒的父母。她去世於1137年在英國英格蘭諾福爾可享年38歲。

nameMeanings.name: Talbot
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Emile, Marcel, Anatole, Armand, Aubert, Camille, Gilles, Normand, Adrien, Alcide, Andre, Auguste.
English and Irish (of Norman origin); French: from the Anglo-Norman French personal name Talebot, a pet form of Old French Talebert, Talbert (see Talbert 1). Compare Talbert 2.
French: from the ancient Germanic personal name Talbald, composed of the elements dal ‘bright, proud’ or tal ‘valley’ + bald ‘bold’.

variants.name: Talbert
meanings: English and Scottish (of Norman origin); French: from the Old French personal name Talebert, Talbert, from ancient Germanic Talaberht or Dalbert, composed of the elements dal ‘bright, proud’ or tal ‘valley’ + berht ‘bright’.
Altered form of English   Talbot  , a cognate of 1 above.

variants.name: Talbott
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Talbot  .

nameMeanings.name: Sybil
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From the name (Greek Sibylla or Sybilla, with confusion over the vowels from an early period) of a class of ancient prophetesses inspired by Apollo. According to medieval theology, they were pagans denied the knowledge of Christ but blessed by God with some insight into things to come and accordingly admitted to heaven. It was thus regarded as a respectable name to be borne by Christians. This name was introduced to England by the Normans and enjoyed considerable popularity in the Middle Ages.

variants.name: Sybilla
meanings: From the name (Greek Sibylla or Sybilla, with confusion over the vowels from an early period) of a class of ancient prophetesses inspired by Apollo. According to medieval theology, they were pagans denied the knowledge of Christ but blessed by God with some insight into things to come and accordingly admitted to heaven. It was thus regarded as a respectable name to be borne by Christians. This name was introduced to England by the Normans and enjoyed considerable popularity in the Middle Ages.

en.lifeSummary: When Sybil Talbot was born about 1100, in Ewias, Herefordshire, England, United Kingdom, her father, Geoffrey I Talbot of Swanscombe, was 32 and her mother, Agnes de Lacy, was 26. She married Payn fitz John about 1125. They were the parents of at least 2 daughters. She died in 1137, in Norfolk, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 38.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sybil Talbot est née environ 1100, à Ewias, Herefordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Geoffrey I Talbot of Swanscombe, avait 32 ans et sa mère, Agnes de Lacy, avait 26 ans. Elle a épousé Payn fitz John environ 1125. Ils ont eu au moins 2 filles. Elle est décédée en 1137, à Norfolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 38 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sybil Talbot um 1100 in Ewias, Herefordshire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Geoffrey I Talbot of Swanscombe 32 und ihre Mutter Agnes de Lacy 26. Sie hat um 1125 Payn fitz John geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Töchter. Sie starb 1137 im Alter von 38 Jahren in Norfolk, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sybil Talbot è nata il circa 1100 nel Ewias, Herefordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, sua padre, Geoffrey I Talbot of Swanscombe, aveva 32 anni e sua madre, Agnes de Lacy, aveva 26 anni. Lei sposato Payn fitz John circa 1125. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figlie. Morì nel 1137, nel Norfolk, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 38 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Sybil Talbot(이)가 대략 1100년 영국 잉글랜드 허퍼드실르 위아스에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Geoffrey I Talbot of Swanscombe(은)는 32세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Agnes de Lacy(은)는 26세였다. 그녀는 대략 1125년 Payn fitz John(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1137년 영국 잉글랜드 노르폴크에서 38세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sybil Talbot nasceu aproximadamente 1100, em Ewias, Herefordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Geoffrey I Talbot of Swanscombe, tinha 32 anos e sua mãe, Agnes de Lacy, tinha 26 anos. Ela casou-se com Payn fitz John aproximadamente 1125. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em 1137, em Norfolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 38 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sybil Talbot родилась около 1100 г. в местности ювяс, хирефордшир, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Geoffrey I Talbot of Swanscombe было 32 года, а матери, которую звали Agnes de Lacy, 26. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Payn fitz John около 1125 г. У них было по крайней мере 2 дочери. Она умерла в 1137 г. в местности норфолк, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 38 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sybil Talbot nació aproximadamente 1100, en Ewias, Herefordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Geoffrey I Talbot of Swanscombe, tenía 32 años y su mamá, Agnes de Lacy, tenía 26 años. Se casó con Payn fitz John aproximadamente 1125. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijas. Murió en 1137, en Norfolk, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 38 años.
